channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Evaluating Large Language Models in a Complex Hidden Role Game
item.description: arXiv:2605.22826v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantifying the deceptive potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) is critical for AI safety, yet difficult to achieve in uncontrolled environments. This work investigates the reasoning, persuasion, and deceptive capabilities of LLMs within the socia...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, framework, prompt, chain-of-thought, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Query-Adaptive Semantic Chunking for Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Dynamic Strategy with Contextual Window Expansion
item.description: arXiv:2605.22834v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems depend critically on document chunking quality for retrieving relevant context. Fixed chunking segments documents into uniform units irrespective of semantics or user intent, producing a precision-recall tr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented, embedding, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Knowledge Distillation for Low-Resource Open-source Text-to-SQL Model
item.description: arXiv:2605.22843v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-SQL converts natural language questions into executable SQL queries, enabling non-technical users to access relational databases for analytics and intelligent data services. In real-world scenarios, performance is often constrained by low-reso...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, prompt, example, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Far Will They Go? Red-Teaming Online Influence with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.22880v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language model (LLM)-based agents increasingly participate in online discourse, red-teaming their capacity to support political influence campaigns is critical for information integrity. In pursuit of this goal, we focus on locally deployed o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, alignment, framework, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RAS: Reflection-Augmented Scaling with In-Context Learning for Executable Cypher Query Generation
item.description: arXiv:2605.22937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Inference-time scaling can reduce errors in structured query generation, but methods to allocate the compute for query code generation remains underexplored. We study Text2Cypher, where language models generate Cypher queries that execute against prop...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, in-context, augmented, study, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learnability-Informed Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.22939v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We aim to improve the reasoning capabilities of diffusion language models (DLMs). While SFT is a popular post-training recipe for autoregressive models, its use in DLMs faces challenges and can even hurt performance, though the underlying causes remai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, reasoning, analysis, ICL, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph Alignment Topology as an Inductive Bias for Grounding Detection
item.description: arXiv:2605.22963v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are optimized to produce distributionally plausible continuations rather than to explicitly verify whether generated propositions are entailed by source documents. This inductive bias enables generalization, but it does no...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, RAG, LLM, retrieval, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can AI Guess What You Know? Performance Comparison of Large Language Models for Human Domain Knowledge Estimation From Communication Logs
item.description: arXiv:2605.22971v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Employees often struggle to identify ``who knows what,'' leading to organizational productivity losses. We investigate whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can infer individual domain knowledge directly from long-term Slack logs. Analyzing 27,188 mess...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, GPT, zero-shot, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When AI Takes Sides on Questions of Faith: Persistent Asymmetries in AI-Mediated Faith Guidance
item.description: arXiv:2605.22975v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We ask whether large language models (LLMs) treat queries about religious conversion symmetrically. The answer is no. When asked for advice on hypothetical faith transitions from one religion to another, then asked the reversed question, models exhibi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, LLM, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BOHM: Zero-Cost Hierarchical Attribution for Compound AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22866v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Compound AI systems route tasks through hierarchies of specialised components. Attribution is dominated by Shapley-based methods (SHAP), which decompose a coalition value function into per-component marginal contributions and require evaluation of the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, RAG, LLM, tool, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RMA: an Agentic System for Research-Level Mathematical Problems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22875v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present $\textbf{Research Math Agents (RMA)}$, an agentic framework for automated reasoning on research-level mathematical problems. Unlike prior studies centered on competition mathematics or formal theorem proving, RMA targets research-level math...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, GPT, reasoning, research, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
item.description: arXiv:2605.22878v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The exponential growth of global academic output has confronted researchers and AI agents with an unprecedented ``information explosion,'' where fragmented and unstructured knowledge organization impedes deep interdisciplinary integration. Current aca...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, release, retrieval, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Deterministic Horizon: Impossibility Results as Design Specifications for Trustworthy AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.23024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models now write software, draft legal documents, and produce clinical notes, yet fundamental limits, from Turing and Arrow to the No Free Lunch theorems, shape what computation can do. This thesis turns such impossibility results from ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, prompt, retrieval, transformer, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PathCal: State-Aware Reflection-Marker Calibration for Efficient Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.23074v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The emergence of Large Reasoning Language Models (LRMs) has paved the way for tackling complex reasoning tasks through test-time scaling by generating long-form Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trajectories during inference. Meanwhile, these trajectories often ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, chain-of-thought, vision, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Latent Cache Flow: Model-to-Model Communication Without Text
item.description: arXiv:2605.22863v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents today communicate via text, which incurs considerable latency and information loss due to the need to autoregressively decode the sharer model's state and encode at the receiver model. Recent work such as Cache-to-Cache (C2C; Fu et al., 202...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, LLM, arxiv, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FuRA: Full-Rank Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Spectral Preconditioning
item.description: arXiv:2605.22869v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Both full fine-tuning (Full FT) and parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods such as LoRA introduce weight updates without accounting for the spectral structure established during pretraining. As a result, noisy gradients from limited fine-tuning data ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, framework, LLM, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Readout Shortcut: Positional Number Copying Dominates Arithmetic CoT Readout in Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.22870v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting is necessary for arithmetic in small language models, yet shuffling its steps preserves most performance. What does CoT contribute if not logical sequencing? In three 1-3B instruction-tuned LMs on GSM8K, we isolate the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, prompt, chain-of-thought, prompting, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Do LLMs Reason? A Dynamical Systems View via Entropy Phase Transitions
item.description: arXiv:2605.22873v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has become the default strategy for enhancing LLM capabilities, yet its application raises a fundamental question: when is explicit reasoning actually beneficial? Empirical evidence reveals a striking paradox: CoT ofte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, chain-of-thought, LLM, zero-shot | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: WeCon: An Efficient Weight-Conditioned Neural Solver for Multi-Objective Combinatorial Optimization Problems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22876v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing neural solvers for Multi-Objective Combinatorial Optimization Problems (MOCOPs) commonly adopt decomposition-based strategies that scalarize an MOCOP into multiple subproblems associated with distinct weight vectors. However, they either inje...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, arxiv, context, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Tensor Cache: Eviction-conditioned Associative Memory for Transformers
item.description: arXiv:2605.22884v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autoregressive Transformer KV caches grow linearly with context length; sliding-window caching bounds memory but discards evicted tokens entirely, so relevant evidence outside the window becomes inaccessible. We introduce \emph{Tensor Cache}, a two-le...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, product, attention, arxiv, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, prompt, context, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.
item.description: A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, LLM, tool, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, retrieval, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, LLM, retrieval, knowledge base | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: NeuroNL2LTL: A Neurosymbolic Framework for Natural Language Translation of Linear Temporal Logic
item.description: arXiv:2605.22874v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effectively translating between natural language (NL) and formal logics like Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) requires expertise that limits formal verification's reach in safety-critical development. Template-based approaches sacrifice expressiveness for ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, template, tool, ICL, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Energy per Successful Goal: Goal-Level Energy Accounting for Agentic AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.22883v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current AI energy benchmarks measure consumption at the granularity of a single model invocation or training run. For classical single-turn workloads this unit remains coherent. For agentic systems - where a single user goal may trigger multi-step orc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, LLM, tool, reasoning, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MedExpMem: Adapting Experience Memory for Differential Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2605.22872v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Experienced physicians develop diagnostic expertise through clinical practice, acquiring not only disease knowledge but also the ability to differentiate confusable conditions. Current medical vision-language models (VLMs) lack this capability -- thei...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, vision, retrieval, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: A Survey of Text and Speech Resources for Hausa and Fongbe: Availability, Quality, and Gaps for NLP Development
item.description: arXiv:2605.22828v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This survey provides a comprehensive catalog of publicly available text and speech resources for two West African languages: Hausa, an Afroasiatic language with approximately 80-100 million speakers, and Fongbe, a Niger-Congo language spoken by approx...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, platform, ICL, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Approximate Machine Unlearning through Manifold Representation Forgetting Guided by Self Mode Connectivity
item.description: arXiv:2605.22871v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine unlearning is a fundamental mechanism that enforces the right to be forgotten. Existing unlearning studies that rely on label manipulation or task-gradient reversal often deliver limited unlearning effectiveness. Moreover, they can undermine t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, experiment, arxiv, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.
item.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, prompt, prompt engineering, tool, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: WrenAI - Give AI agents the context to query business data correctly through the open context layer that gives AI agents grounded, governed memory, context, SQL across 20+ data sources, that helps you build GenBI, agentic BI, text-to-sql, dashboards, and agentic analytics.

item.title: ImProver 2: Iteratively Self-Improving LMs for Neurosymbolic Proof Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.22885v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formal mathematics libraries are rapidly expanding, creating a growing need to refactor verified proofs for maintainability and to improve training data quality for neural provers. However, scalable proof optimization is hindered by heterogeneous and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, framework, research, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: Mediative Fuzzy Logic: From Type-1 Foundations to Type-2, Type-3 and Quantum Extensions
item.description: arXiv:2605.22900v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mediative Fuzzy Logic was conceived as a practical scheme for reconciling hesitant or conflicting assessments in fuzzy control and decision-making. However, its logical and semantic foundations remain underdeveloped, especially beyond operational type...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, example, ICL, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: EVE-Agent: Evidence-Verifiable Self-Evolving Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.22905v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-evolving agents should not train on examples they cannot justify. Data-free self-evolving search agents offer a scalable route to systems that generate their own questions, answer them, and improve from their own feedback without human annotation...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, example, model, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: FusionSense: Tri-Stage Near-Sensor Learning for Runtime-Adaptive Multimodal Edge Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2605.22868v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous systems and smart-industry deployments increasingly split computation across near-sensor, edge, and cloud resources, where tight energy, latency, and reliability budgets demand run-time adaptivity.
